nodes.description: Multiple accessory oral frenula (bands of tissue between lips, gums, and tongue) are a hallmark oral feature.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations including deletions of the entire OFD1 gene in 30 families with type 1 orofaciodigital syndrome: a study of the extensive clinical variability.
evidence.snippet: oral features are the most reliable diagnostic criteria

nodes.description: Agenesis or dysgenesis of the corpus callosum is a common CNS malformation.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations including deletions of the entire OFD1 gene in 30 families with type 1 orofaciodigital syndrome: a study of the extensive clinical variability.
evidence.snippet: complete agenesis of the corpus callosum, large single or multiple interhemispheric cysts, striking cortical infolding of gyri, ventriculomegaly, mild molar tooth malformation and moderate to severe cerebellar vermis hypoplasia

evidence.reference_title: Central nervous system malformations and early end-stage renal disease in oro-facio-digital syndrome type I: a review.
evidence.snippet: Intracerebral cysts and porencephaly or arachnoid cysts are rarely but are repeatedly reported in orofaciodigital (OFD) syndrome type 1

nodes.description: Autistic features and attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder behaviors have been recognized in OFD1 patients, warranting behavioral assessment and standard interventions.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: standard treatments for attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder and/or autistic features

nodes.description: Short digits (brachydactyly) are a recognized digital abnormality in OFD1, often co-occurring with syndactyly and clinodactyly.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: digital features (brachydactyly, syndactyly, clinodactyly of the fifth finger, duplicated great toe)

nodes.description: Hypoplasia of the cerebellar vermis, ranging from mild to severe, is a brain MRI finding in OFD1 patients with CNS involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations including deletions of the entire OFD1 gene in 30 families with type 1 orofaciodigital syndrome: a study of the extensive clinical variability.
evidence.snippet: complete brain phenotype consisting of complete agenesis of the corpus callosum, large single or multiple interhemispheric cysts, striking cortical infolding of gyri, ventriculomegaly, mild molar tooth malformation and moderate to severe cerebellar vermis hypoplasia

nodes.description: Cleft of the hard or soft palate occurs in a proportion of patients and contributes to feeding difficulties and speech problems.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: OFD1 is characterized by the following: oral features (lobulated tongue, tongue nodules, cleft of the hard or soft palate, accessory gingival frenulae, hypodontia, and other dental abnormalities)

nodes.description: Incurving of the fifth finger (clinodactyly) is a recognized digital abnormality in OFD1.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: digital features (brachydactyly, syndactyly, clinodactyly of the fifth finger, duplicated great toe)

nodes.description: Cerebellar agenesis with or without Dandy-Walker malformation occurs in a proportion of OFD1 patients with brain MRI abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: brain MRI findings (intracerebral cysts, agenesis of the corpus callosum, cerebellar agenesis with or without Dandy-Walker malformation)

nodes.description: Duplication of the hallux (great toe) is a distinctive digital finding in OFD1, reported as a hallmark feature in the GeneReviews clinical description.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: digital features (brachydactyly, syndactyly, clinodactyly of the fifth finger, duplicated great toe)

nodes.description: Widely spaced eyes (hypertelorism/telecanthus) are a characteristic facial feature, often present with other midface anomalies.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: facial features (widely spaced eyes, telecanthus, hypoplasia of the alae nasi, median cleft or pseudocleft of the upper lip, micrognathia)

nodes.description: Missing teeth (hypodontia) and other dental abnormalities including delayed eruption and supernumerary teeth are documented in OFD1.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: OFD1 is characterized by the following: oral features (lobulated tongue, tongue nodules, cleft of the hard or soft palate, accessory gingival frenulae, hypodontia, and other dental abnormalities)

nodes.description: Intellectual disability of variable severity occurs in approximately 50% of affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations including deletions of the entire OFD1 gene in 30 families with type 1 orofaciodigital syndrome: a study of the extensive clinical variability.
evidence.snippet: A first, detailed evaluation of brain MRIs from seven patients with cognitive defects illustrated extensive variability

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, molecular, and genotype-phenotype correlation studies from 25 cases of oral-facial-digital syndrome type 1: a French and Belgian collaborative study.
evidence.snippet: mental retardation and mutations located in exons 3, 8, 9, 13, and 16

nodes.description: Lobulated or multilobed tongue with hamartomas is a characteristic oral finding.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, molecular, and genotype-phenotype correlation studies from 25 cases of oral-facial-digital syndrome type 1: a French and Belgian collaborative study.
evidence.snippet: lingual hamartomas were significantly more frequent in the group with OFD1 mutation

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations including deletions of the entire OFD1 gene in 30 families with type 1 orofaciodigital syndrome: a study of the extensive clinical variability.
evidence.snippet: oral features are the most reliable diagnostic criteria

nodes.description: Median (midline) cleft of the upper lip, distinct from the more common lateral cleft lip.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, molecular, and genotype-phenotype correlation studies from 25 cases of oral-facial-digital syndrome type 1: a French and Belgian collaborative study.
evidence.snippet: facial dysmorphism with oral, tooth, and distal abnormalities

nodes.description: Small mandible (micrognathia) is part of the characteristic facial dysmorphism in OFD1.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: facial features (widely spaced eyes, telecanthus, hypoplasia of the alae nasi, median cleft or pseudocleft of the upper lip, micrognathia)

nodes.description: Polycystic kidney disease develops in a significant proportion of patients, potentially leading to renal failure.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, molecular, and genotype-phenotype correlation studies from 25 cases of oral-facial-digital syndrome type 1: a French and Belgian collaborative study.
evidence.snippet: polycystic kidney disease, and central nervous system malformations

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, molecular, and genotype-phenotype correlation studies from 25 cases of oral-facial-digital syndrome type 1: a French and Belgian collaborative study.
evidence.snippet: polycystic kidneys and short stature were significantly more frequent in the group with no OFD1 mutation

evidence.reference_title: Central nervous system malformations and early end-stage renal disease in oro-facio-digital syndrome type I: a review.
evidence.snippet: renal cystic disease is an often overlooked complication specific to OFD 1

nodes.description: Digital anomalies including brachydactyly, clinodactyly, syndactyly, and polydactyly affecting hands and/or feet.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, molecular, and genotype-phenotype correlation studies from 25 cases of oral-facial-digital syndrome type 1: a French and Belgian collaborative study.
evidence.snippet: facial dysmorphism with oral, tooth, and distal abnormalities

evidence.reference_title: The molecular basis of oral-facial-digital syndrome, type 1.
evidence.snippet: OFD1 is characterized by malformation of the oral cavity, face, and digits

nodes.description: OFD1 encodes a centrosomal and basal body protein required for primary cilium formation. OFD1 is involved in centriole distal appendage assembly and ciliogenesis. Loss of function disrupts Hedgehog and WNT signaling pathways dependent on primary cilia, leading to defects in craniofacial, limb, brain, and kidney development.

evidence.reference_title: The molecular basis of oral-facial-digital syndrome, type 1.
evidence.snippet: OFD1 gene that encodes a centrosomal protein localized at the basal bodies at the origin of primary cilia

evidence.reference_title: The molecular basis of oral-facial-digital syndrome, type 1.
evidence.snippet: Ofd1 inactivation is associated to defective sonic hedgehog (Shh) and canonical Wnt signaling pathways

evidence.reference_title: The molecular basis of oral-facial-digital syndrome, type 1.
evidence.snippet: OFD1 has a crucial role in the biology of primary cilia thus ascribing this pleiotropic disease to the growing number of disorders associated to dysfunction of primary cilia

nodes.description: Disruption of primary cilia function in renal tubular epithelium leads to polycystic kidney disease, similar to other ciliopathies. Renal involvement is progressive and may lead to renal failure.

evidence.reference_title: Central nervous system malformations and early end-stage renal disease in oro-facio-digital syndrome type I: a review.
evidence.snippet: cystic medullary disease was noted in OFD 1 carriers, leading 1 patient to dialysis by age 35 years and the other to severe renal insufficiency by age 28 years

evidence.reference_title: The molecular basis of oral-facial-digital syndrome, type 1.
evidence.snippet: OFD1 shares phenotypic similarities with this latter group of disorders, such as cystic kidneys, skeletal, and CNS abnormalities

nodes.description: Fusion of adjacent digits (syndactyly) affects hands and/or feet in a subset of OFD1 patients.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: digital features (brachydactyly, syndactyly, clinodactyly of the fifth finger, duplicated great toe)

nodes.description: Benign hamartomas of the tongue are a highly characteristic oral finding in OFD1, often referred to as tongue nodules or lingual hamartomas.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: OFD1 is characterized by the following: oral features (lobulated tongue, tongue nodules, cleft of the hard or soft palate, accessory gingival frenulae, hypodontia, and other dental abnormalities)

nodes.description: Hypoplasia of the nasal alae (wings of the nostrils) is a recognized midface feature associated with the craniofacial ciliopathy phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Oral-Facial-Digital Syndrome Type I.
evidence.snippet: facial features (widely spaced eyes, telecanthus, hypoplasia of the alae nasi, median cleft or pseudocleft of the upper lip, micrognathia)

nodes.description: Enlargement of the cerebral ventricles (ventriculomegaly) is seen on brain MRI in patients with more severe CNS involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations including deletions of the entire OFD1 gene in 30 families with type 1 orofaciodigital syndrome: a study of the extensive clinical variability.
evidence.snippet: complete brain phenotype consisting of complete agenesis of the corpus callosum, large single or multiple interhemispheric cysts, striking cortical infolding of gyri, ventriculomegaly, mild molar tooth malformation and moderate to severe cerebellar vermis hypoplasia
